steps.title: Name the specific lions
steps.content: The names Ginger and Shaka identify which lions in the Matimba coalition are being discussed. Because the names are essential to specifying the subject, they should not be set off with commas.

steps.title: Reject appositive commas
steps.content: Choice B treats the names as a nonessential aside. Choice C places a comma between the complete subject and the verb "prowled." Choice D opens an appositive structure with a comma but never closes it. The no-comma form keeps the identifying names attached. The answer is A.
